name: The Rotting Hollow
description: Below the canopy the ground sinks into a wide, sunken hollow where the garden turns to open decay. The springing fungal mat gives way to a bog of black rot, ankle-deep and warm, its surface skinned over with films of grey mould that tear and knit as you wade through them. Half-dissolved shapes rise out of the muck — the swallowed dead of a hundred fallen things, held upright by the growth that has claimed them and slowly unmade into nutrient and spore. The reek here is overpowering, a wet green rot that coats the back of the throat, and the very air seems to work against you, thick with a poison the Bloom breathes out as freely as a forest breathes shade. Every surface glistens, every surface is faintly, horribly warm, and nothing here is quite finished dying.
ambient_messages: A skin of grey mould tears across the bog with a wet sigh and knits itself shut behind you.
A half-dissolved shape sinks a little further into the black rot, and the muck closes over it with a swallow.
The poison-thick air thickens further, and your breath comes shallow and sweetish in the reek.
